title: New York Comic Con screening 30 on October 11th
display_title: New York Comic Con screening 30 on October 11th
wikitext:
[]: New York Comic Con screening 3.0 on October 11th             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jun 14, 2013 1:01 pm http://www.evageeks.org/2013/06/evangelion-3-0-screening-at-new-york-comicon/ 2 threads about NYCC were merged, one from June and another from July. Excuse any mess. -SSD

[]: Re: New York Comic Con screening 3.0 on October 11th             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Jun 14, 2013 1:55 pm Ornette wrote: http://www.evageeks.org/2013/06/evangelion-3-0-screening-at-new-york-comicon/

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jun 14, 2013 1:58 pm ...well, get ready for more cases of post-q-catatonia. *prepares beds*

[]:             Quote Post by Ænimal » Fri Jun 14, 2013 2:00 pm Kendrix wrote: ...well, get ready for more cases of post-q-catatonia. *prepares beds*

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jun 14, 2013 2:13 pm Ænimal wrote: but damn you, New York!!! why can't they have stuff like this where i live?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Jun 14, 2013 2:18 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: You're not missing much. I went to Comic Con 2011 as a panelist. It sucked.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jun 14, 2013 2:21 pm I don't know how these screenings are run, but there was one year that I went to see a NYCC screening of Genius Party and it was just at a venue. I didn't have to deal with the con at all, just showed up at the venue and bought a ticket. Not sure if that was just something special.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Jun 14, 2013 2:22 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: You're not missing much. I went to Comic Con 2011 as a panelist. It sucked.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jun 14, 2013 2:25 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: I find that some years are good, some not as good. I remember enjoying 2010 a lot more than 2012.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Jun 14, 2013 2:27 pm riffraff11235 wrote: I'm curious to see how that large group of fans will react to the film.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Fri Jun 14, 2013 2:46 pm Ornette wrote: I don't know how these screenings are run, but there was one year that I went to see a NYCC screening of Genius Party and it was just at a venue. I didn't have to deal with the con at all, just showed up at the venue and bought a ticket. Not sure if that was just something special.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Jun 15, 2013 7:55 pm Oh good, I'm hoping everyone will be at least be surprised as to what happens in this film. To have it shown at convention makes the reactions Glorious!

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sat Jun 15, 2013 8:00 pm riffraff11235 wrote: I had fun at NYCC 2011, but I guess that's partly because it was my first con and I had nothing to compare to. 2012 wasn't bad either. I'll definitely be attending the screening if I make it to NYCC this year. I'm curious to see how that large group of fans will react to the film.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Jun 15, 2013 8:15 pm CJD wrote: riff if you go you need to take a camera, or use your phone assuming it has the capability, and interview a bunch of people as they walk out of the theater. Find people who hadn't read spoilers or seen the movie before, I want to see their reactions in person. For science!

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sat Jun 15, 2013 8:42 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: The only thing that could possibly get me to go back is if they lucked out and got Anno or Sadamoto as a guest.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sat Jun 15, 2013 9:24 pm I don't know if it's that he doesn't like America (not that I care either way). It's probably more that if there's anything worse than "mouth-breathing fans who aren't happy with the storyline you gave them because they unjustly feel they deserve a different one" it's "mouth-breathing fans who aren't happy with the storyline you gave them because they unjustly feel they deserve a different one who speak a different language". If I was him I wouldn't want to associate with that crowd either. 90% of the questions would fall along the lines of "Why you no like Asuka anyway" or "Why is Shinji - deep intake of breath, wipes sweat off fat brow - such a stupid pussy? It all woulda been - takes deep soggy gulp from 106oz soda - better if Shinji was all gar."

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Jun 15, 2013 9:33 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: 90% of the questions would fall along the lines of "Why you no like Asuka anyway" or "Why is Shinji - deep intake of breath, wipes sweat off fat brow - such a stupid pussy? It all woulda been - takes deep soggy gulp from 106oz soda - better if Shinji was all gar."

[]:             Quote Post by ancapevageek » Sun Jun 16, 2013 3:41 am will be there. get at me.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Jun 16, 2013 12:25 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: It's probably more that if there's anything worse than "mouth-breathing fans who aren't happy with the storyline you gave them because they unjustly feel they deserve a different one" it's "mouth-breathing fans who aren't happy with the storyline you gave them because they unjustly feel they deserve a different one who speak a different language".

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jun 16, 2013 1:16 pm Guy Nacks wrote: Sadamoto could possibly be a guest, but Anno... heh... no chance in hell. It just sounds as though the guy doesn't really like America all that much.
